summary: Package hijacks console.warn/console.error at require time to exfiltrate log data to a hardcoded Telegram bot
details: On require(), index.js runs an IIFE that replaces globalThis.console.warn and console.error with async wrappers that POST their first argument to https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted-token>/sendMessage with hardcoded chat_ids (-1001161709623 for warn, -1001433099398 for error). The original console methods are not invoked, so the installer's application-level log output — frequently containing stack traces, file paths, environment details, and occasionally secrets — is silently redirected to an attacker-controlled Telegram channel for every process that loads this module. This behavior is undocumented, triggered purely by importing the package, and directly harms any installer who integrates it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
